steps.title: Identify the tangency condition
steps.content:
A circle intersects the y-axis at exactly one point when it is tangent to the y-axis. This occurs when the distance from the circle's center to the y-axis equals the circle's radius.

For a circle $(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$:
  • Center: $(h, k)$
  • Radius: $r$
  • Distance from center to y-axis: $|h|$
We need: $|h| = r$

steps.title: Find which circle is tangent to the y-axis
steps.content:
All four choices have $r^2 = 16$, so the radius is $r = 4$. We need $|h| = 4$.
  • A: $(x-8)^2 + (y-8)^2 = 16$ has center $(8, 8)$, so $|h| = 8 eq 4$
  • B: $(x-8)^2 + (y-4)^2 = 16$ has center $(8, 4)$, so $|h| = 8 eq 4$
  • C: $(x-4)^2 + (y-9)^2 = 16$ has center $(4, 9)$, so $|h| = 4$, which equals $r$ ✓
  • D: $x^2 + (y-9)^2 = 16$ has center $(0, 9)$, so $|h| = 0 eq 4$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
